page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-90-lssd is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 90 vCPUs, which correspond to 45 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled at 2.0 threads per core. The system is equipped with 360 GB of RAM, providing a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 3216 GB of NVMe SSD capacity distributed across 8 local SSDs.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with the stress-ng multi-core test and PassMark CPU score ranking in the top 10%. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI, which contributes to top-tier performance in PassMark CPU extended instructions and encryption tests. Single-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core but dropping to average in Geekbench single-core tests. Memory bandwidth is strong for read operations, reaching 6372.53 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 217.44 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, though large block write bandwidth is average at 76.89 GB/sec.

This server configuration is suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving. Redis SET operations achieve top-tier throughput at 117.73 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is strong at 56.29 GB/sec. For machine learning workloads, the instance achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds of 4127.91 tokens per second with small 135M models, though text generation speeds for larger 70B models remain average at 4.79 tokens per second due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. The combination of high-density local NVMe storage and balanced compute resources offers qualitative cost efficiency for I/O-intensive workloads that require high local read/write performance without the overhead of external storage attachments.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-90-lssd is a general-purpose server instance featuring 90 vCPUs across 45 physical AMD EPYC 9B14 cores and 360 GB of RAM. It includes 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 8 drives. The x86_64 architecture supports AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration, enabling top-tier multi-core CPU performance and encryption capabilities. Benchmarks show strong memory read bandwidth alongside top-tier Redis SET operations and software compilation speeds. While single-core and large-block write performance are average, the instance delivers strong database operations and static web serving throughput. This configuration is highly suited for database hosting, web serving, and parallelized build systems that benefit from high-density local storage and balanced compute resources.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-90-lssd is a general-purpose server with 90 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 360 GB RAM, and 3216 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-90-lssd features 90 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 360 GB RAM, and 3216 GB local NVMe storage for database and compute workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring ninety AMD EPYC vCPUs, high-speed local NVMe storage, and top-tier multi-core processing capabilities.
bullet_points: 90 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors with AVX-512 support
360 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per vCPU
3216 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage across 8 drives
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and cryptographic execution
Strong database operations, Redis throughput, and static web serving
